fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Improving responder access and movement on the property could be achieved by managing the dense vegetation surrounding the main building and landscaped areas. Thinning or trimming the vegetation could facilitate easier navigation for emergency personnel, enhance visibility, and reduce potential obstacles. Enhancing side access to the property by widening entry points or providing alternative routes may support effective maneuvering and staging of emergency vehicles around the building. By ensuring that pathways are clearly defined and unobstructed, emergency operations could be executed more swiftly and efficiently, enabling responders to reach critical areas without delay.

wildfire.conditions: Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Tree Cluster; Vegetated Perimeter; Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Overhang; Fuel Continuity
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could be achieved by selectively thinning areas where a dense tree canopy and tree clusters are present, thereby reducing fuel continuity. Pruning tree branches that overhang the structure and removing or trimming vegetation directly adjacent to the structure and fence could further decrease potential fire exposure. Managing the vegetated perimeter by creating clearings or fuel breaks at strategic locations can enhance fuel separation and limit fire spread towards the structures. Improving the spacing and separation of scattered trees would also contribute to reducing fire intensity if a wildfire were to occur. By focusing on these measures, property preparedness for potential wildfire impacts could be significantly strengthened, supporting long-term resilience and making the environment more conducive to effective firefighting efforts. Moreover, maintaining any open areas and ensuring that access routes remain clear of vegetation will provide critical support for suppression operations and firefighter access.
